text.title: Technical Debt Trade-off Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every debt-record must obey. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every debt-record MUST tag both axes of the Fowler quadrant: intent (deliberate|inadvertent) and prudence (prudent|reckless). Untagged or single-axis records are invalid.
rule.rationale: The two axes determine repayment strategy: deliberate-prudent debt is a trade; inadvertent-reckless is a flaw. Conflating them produces wrong fixes.

rule.statement: repayment_trigger MUST be an observable signal (metric + threshold + measurement source), not a phrase containing "someday", "when we have time", or "later". Time-based triggers without a metric are also rejected.
rule.rationale: Without a concrete trigger, debt accumulates indefinitely; backlogs grow until the project collapses under interest.

rule.statement: Adding a new debt item MUST NOT push total debt cost above 20% of project capacity. Over-budget additions are blocked; existing debt must be repaid first.
rule.rationale: Teams crossing 25% debt allocation halve their feature delivery rate; 15-20% is the empirically observed sustainable band.

rule.statement: Severity MUST be tagged: localized (one module/file) or systemic (touches ≥3 modules or a foundation choice). Systemic debt requires ATAM-grade review, not a debt-record.
rule.rationale: Systemic debt is a quality-attribute risk, not a backlog item; treating it as backlog hides the real cost.

rule.statement: If the "better solution" was unknown at the time of the shortcut, the record is inadvertent — NOT debt; it is a learning. Backfilling deliberate-prudent classification on retro-discovered shortcuts is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Classification fraud destroys the value of the quadrant; if everything becomes "deliberate-prudent" the team loses the signal that distinguishes trade from mistake.
